tasks.title: Enforce CONFIRMED gate -- kb_capture caps at INFERRED (F1)
tasks.notes: Clamp enforced in kbCapture handler (server-side): CONFIRMED->INFERRED, confidence_clamped flag + bracketed content note. kb_promote unchanged. D6 user-directive exemption via raw-string check (TODO T3.1). Tool desc updated in index.ts. Forward-only, no row migration. Fail-then-pass: 3 failed on old code, all 4 pass now.

tasks.title: Trust-model docs + tpl-kb-agent update (F1)
tasks.notes: Docs only. New docs/kb-trust-model.md (short). tpl-kb-agent.md: knowledge capture at INFERRED then promote, confidence decision table reworked to Capture-at/Then columns matching the clamp, capture step + rules updated, D6 note added. ASCII clean.

tasks.title: Supersede for real -- superseded_at + stale=1 on update (F2)
tasks.notes: evaluateAudn 'update' branch now sets superseded_at=now AND stale=1 on old entry; content_hash intact; flagged/none untouched. New kb-supersede.test.ts (fail-then-pass: stale assertion failed on old code, passes now). Adjusted existing kb-capture.test.ts update test to pass include_stale for the now-stale old row. Full knowledge suite 123 pass.

tasks.title: Loosen contradiction flagging + cross-type candidate discovery (F2)
tasks.notes: HALF A: makeAudnDecision flags on symbol overlap + contradiction signal regardless of file/type; signal = extended CONTRADICTION_KEYWORDS OR new hasOppositePolarity() antonym check; dedup/update re-impose same-type + file overlap (cross-type only for contradiction). HALF B: removed 'AND e.type = ?' from findAudnCandidates (now cross-type discovery). Pure fail-then-pass in audn.test.ts (3 failed on old, pass now): code_graph pair + cross-type -> flagged; no-signal -> not flagged; cross-type same-file -> no update; same-type dedup regression green. Suite 128 pass.

tasks.title: FTS OR-join across 4 sites + F2 cross-type contradiction e2e (F4)
tasks.notes: Extracted shared exported helper orJoinFtsTerms (+ftsSafeTerm) in audn.ts; applied at all 4 implicit-AND sites: audn.ts makeFtsQuery (CHANGED per D4, feeds findAudnCandidates), sqlite-provider.ts prime() searchTerms, kb-session-prime.ts neighbor batch + global-append (also now sanitizes raw session_files paths via ftsSafeTerm, closing the FTS-hostile-path throw noted in feedback.md finding 3). Removed the now-duplicate local ftsSafeTerm from kb-session-prime.ts. Ranking/include_stale/l1_only untouched; single-term behavior regression-tested. Fail-then-pass verified via git stash of the 3 source files: all new/changed assertions (orJoinFtsTerms unit tests, prime multi-term retrieval, neighbor/global-append OR-join query-string proofs, F2 e2e) failed on old code, pass on new. F2 e2e (tests/knowledge/kb-fts-orjoin.test.ts): capture cross-type contradiction (knowledge 'code_graph is broken' then learning 'code_graph now works...', no shared file) -> flagged + contradiction_of set; was 'add' pre-T2.1 since T1.4 alone only cleared the type filter, AND-join still blocked FTS discovery. kb_session_prime retrieval check: pre-fix prime with the sprint's hint_symbols/hint_modules returned zero top_entries (confirmed live via kb_session_prime call before starting); this is exactly the AND-join gap T2.1 closes.

tasks.title: Auto-staleness at prime -- capture hash basis + checkFreshness (F3)
tasks.notes: PART A: additive source_file_hashes TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' column (try/catch ALTER TABLE pattern, same as scope); capture() computes computeSourceFileHashes(source_files) via computeFileHashBatch and threads it through insertEntry for ALL types (add/flagged/update branches all pass sourceFileHashes). PART B: new private checkFreshness(db, entries) in SqliteProvider -- entries with source_files AND a non-empty stored basis get re-hashed (ONE computeFileHashBatch over the union of basis files across the primed set) and compared; any changed/missing basis file -> stale=1 (single UPDATE) + dropped from top_entries; empty/no basis entries untouched. Called in prime() after top_entries is built, wrapped in try/catch (graceful degradation to today's output on any error). Fail-then-pass verified via git stash of sqlite-provider.ts (kept T2.1's fix committed): the 'modified file -> stale + dropped' and 'basis persisted' assertions failed on pre-T2.2 code, pass now; the error-degradation and no-basis-untouched assertions already passed on old code (correctly, since old code did nothing) and still pass. Full knowledge suite (24 files, 145 tests) green; build clean.

tasks.title: Provenance enums stamped by tool layer + harvest provenance (F5)
tasks.notes: types.ts: Author (D5 list + 'harvest' ADDED per revised D7 -- DEVIATION from D5's literal enum, needed so harvested entries get a distinct author from real KB-Agent captures per feedback.md LOW finding 6) and CaptureSource (session/review/harvest/promotion/user-directive/unknown) added; KBEntry.source typed CaptureSource | string, author stays string -- tolerant reads, no row migration. kb-capture.ts: removed free-string author/source zod params, added role hint validated against Author (invalid/absent -> 'unknown'); source derived server-side (review for validated reviewer role, user-directive for the existing D6 raw-string exemption, else session) -- callers cannot inject a free string into storage. kb-promote path: sqlite-provider.ts promote() now stamps source='promotion' on the promoted row (touches sqlite-provider.ts beyond T2.3's literal file list, but required for the promote->'promotion' test; also converted the promotion-note template literal to string concatenation per the ASCII hook gotcha). kb-harvest.ts: literals only, author 'kb-harvest'->'harvest', source 'kb_agent_harvest'->'harvest'; confidence untouched (UNVERIFIED). index.ts kb_harvest description updated. Fail-then-pass verified via git stash of all 5 touched source files: valid-role/invalid/absent/reviewer/promote/harvest assertions all failed on pre-fix code, pass now; legacy-value read-back test passed both before and after (tolerant reads, no migration). Full knowledge suite 152 tests green; full npm test 1808 passed / 2 pre-existing yashr-302 timezone failures (allowed) / 14 skipped; build clean.

tasks.title: user-directive entry type at highest trust tier (F6)
tasks.notes: D6 all four semantics landed. (1) types.ts ContentType += 'user-directive'; kb-capture.ts replaces T1.1's raw-string TODO(T3.1) guard with the typed-union check, forces confidence='CONFIRMED' (SOLE clamp exemption), stamps author='user' + source='user-directive'; also added 'user-directive' to the kb-capture zod type enum so captures are accepted. (2) decayConceptEntries gains defensive `AND type != 'user-directive'` (belt-and-braces per D6 -- decay only touches INFERRED, a directive is CONFIRMED; comment states this). (3) makeAudnDecision (audn.ts, pure) supersede guard: `if (candidate.type === 'user-directive' && input.type !== 'user-directive') continue;` placed before the same-type gate -- an agent capture degrades to 'flagged' (contradiction path above) or 'add', never update; both-user-directive passes -> normal supersede (relies on T1.4 cross-type findAudnCandidates, confirmed present). (4) retrieval: CONFIRMED gives CONFIRMED-equiv ranking, no extra code -- comment on ContentType + in kb-capture. Docs: tpl-kb-agent.md (rewrote the stale 'lands in Phase 3' note into a real user-directive section + WHEN-to-record trigger) and skills/pm/SKILL.md (WHEN-to-record paragraph). Tests: new tests/knowledge/kb-user-directive.test.ts, 13 tests: clamp exemption stores CONFIRMED + author/user + source/user-directive (contrast: normal knowledge CONFIRMED clamps to INFERRED); retrievable top-rank via query() and prime(); decay control (INFERRED concept downgraded) + directive survives + type-guard defensive proof (forced-INFERRED directive not downgraded); pure makeAudnDecision guard (add / flagged / both-directive update); e2e capture: agent contradiction flags directive keeping superseded_at NULL + CONFIRMED, second user-directive supersedes (superseded_at + stale). Full knowledge suite 165 green; build clean; ASCII clean.

tasks.title: Keep kb_harvest autowired, make it honest + low-trust (F7)
tasks.notes: execute-prompt.ts autowire (lines ~323-330) unchanged; kb-harvest-autowire.test.ts confirmed still green (3 tests pass). Confirmed harvested entries already forced confidence=UNVERIFIED with author=harvest/source=harvest (set in T2.3) and covered by the D1 clamp -- no source-of-truth change needed, only confirmation + a comment. Removed the redundant 'call kb_harvest yourself at session end' no-op instruction (kbHarvest returns zero counts with no session_transcript, which a manually-invoking agent never has) from tpl-doer.md, tpl-reviewer.md (same pattern found there too, fixed for consistency though not explicitly named in PLAN.md's file list), doer-reviewer-loop.md's doer dispatch template, and skills/fleet/knowledge-agent.md Phase 3 (also not explicitly named but covered by the 'docs referencing harvest' catch-all; same anti-pattern). Added one clarifying line to tpl-kb-agent.md (autowire is a separate low-trust transcript-fed path, direct-capture stays primary) and a doc comment to kb-harvest.ts. docs/kb-trust-model.md already accurately described harvest (autowired/UNVERIFIED/regex-extracted, not dead) -- no change needed. New behavioral test in kb-harvest.test.ts: captures from a fixture transcript, then queries the provider directly and asserts confidence=UNVERIFIED, author=harvest, source=harvest on the resulting entry. Full knowledge suite green (kb-harvest.test.ts 4/4, kb-harvest-autowire.test.ts 3/3); build clean; ASCII clean.

tasks.title: kb_list tool (F8)
tasks.notes: New src/tools/kb-list.ts: input {confidence?, type?, module?, symbol?, limit?} -> {results:[{id,type,confidence,title,summary,symbols,source_files}], total}, routed through providers.project. New SqliteProvider.list() -- CHOICE (stated per PLAN.md): a dedicated provider read method rather than a query() opt-out flag, since query()'s use_count/last_accessed bump is a deliberate retrieval-telemetry contract kb_list should not perturb (pure audit tool). Always excludes superseded/stale (no override). module is exact-match column filter; symbol uses json_each same technique as context() uses for source_files. Registered in src/index.ts with description; added to README.md KB tools table (llms-full.txt auto-regenerated by pre-commit hook). Tests (kb-list.test.ts, 9 tests): confidence/type/module/symbol filters isolate correct rows; limit respected (unbounded when omitted); superseded/stale excluded by default; use_count/last_accessed proven untouched via query({ids}) before/after comparison; tool wrapper returns exactly the stable reduced field set. Full knowledge suite 175 tests green; build clean; ASCII clean (pre-existing non-ASCII byte in index.ts is outside my diff and outside the ASCII-checked file types anyway).

tasks.title: Canonical export .fleet/kb-canonical.json (F8)
tasks.notes: New src/tools/kb-export.ts registered as a real MCP tool (kb_export, per D8's stated decision) so the MCP-only KB Agent can invoke it. Writes all live CONFIRMED project entries (via SqliteProvider.list() from T3.3) to <repo>/.fleet/kb-canonical.json with the stable field set {id,type,title,summary,symbols,source_files,confidence,updated_at}; updated_at = promoted_at falling back to created_at (covers user-directive CONFIRMED-on-capture entries, which have no promoted_at). Deterministic id ordering. ASCII-safe output implemented as a charCodeAt/toString(16) walk (not a regex with a literal unicode-escape range) -- an early attempt using \uXXXX-range regex syntax got corrupted into literal non-ASCII bytes by the Write-tool content path itself, so the final implementation avoids writing any raw unicode escape sequence into the source file at all; also avoids template literals per the known ASCII-hook backtick-n/t/r false positive. repo_path optional input, defaulted to cwd, validated like kb-setup.ts's repo_path (existence + directory check). tpl-kb-agent.md Step 6b (kb_export after promotion, PM commits the file) and skills/pm/SKILL.md Lifecycle section document the flow; README.md tools table updated. Tests (kb-export.test.ts, 7 tests): fixture CONFIRMED+INFERRED+superseded-CONFIRMED -> only live CONFIRMED exported; exact field set; deterministic order over 5 entries; missing .fleet dir created; zero-CONFIRMED exports []; output verified byte-for-byte ASCII with non-ASCII entry text (built via String.fromCharCode at runtime, not typed literally, since this test file must itself stay ASCII); invalid repo_path rejects. Full knowledge suite 182 tests green; build clean; ASCII clean.
